phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest BOJ and ECB monetary policy data. Fetch and report: - BOJ current policy stance + next meeting rate-hike probability (from OIS swap pricing or Bloomberg consensus — exact %) - ECB current policy stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from OIS swap pricing — exact %) - BOJ Policy Rate Expectation: where is the BOJ Policy Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - ECB Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the ECB Deposit Facility Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - Any BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda comments in the last 48 hours — specifically any language around policy normalization, rate-hike timing, or YCC/NIRP exit progress - Any ECB President Christine Lagarde comments in the last 48 hours — specifically any language around rate cuts, inflation path, or growth outlook - BOJ Policy Normalization Status: has BOJ fully exited NIRP (Negative Interest Rate Policy) and YCC (Yield Curve Control)? What is the current stage of normalization? - Which central bank is more hawkish right now and why — note that for EURJPY, a more hawkish BOJ = bearish EURJPY (JPY strengthens), while a more hawkish ECB = bullish EURJPY (EUR strengthens) ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - BOJ Stance: [hawkish/dovish/normalizing] | Hike Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Policy Rate: [X%] - ECB Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Rate Differential by Year-End: [ECB Terminal – BOJ Terminal = bps gap] — widening or narrowing vs current? - BOJ Normalization Stage: [NIRP exited Y/N | YCC abandoned Y/N | Next expected step] - Key Speaker Note — Ueda: [most recent BOJ comment in 48h, or None] - Key Speaker Note — Lagarde: [most recent ECB comment in 48h, or None] - Dominant CB: [BOJ/ECB/Equal] - EURJPY Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - POLICY DIVERGENCE (BOJ vs ECB)] - BOJ Outlook: [stance + hike probability % + year-end policy rate %] - ECB Outlook: [stance + cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - Year-End Rate Gap (ECB–BOJ): [bps, widening/narrowing] - BOJ Normalization Stage: [current status] - Key Speaker Note — Ueda: [comment or None] - Key Speaker Note — Lagarde: [comment or None] - Core Logic: [why this divergence + terminal rate gap matters for EURJPY today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase2: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest Japanese Government Bond (JGB) and German Bund yield data. Fetch and report: - Japan 2Y JGB and 10Y JGB yields (exact %) - Germany 2Y (Schatz) and 10Y (Bund) yields (exact %) - DE-JP 2Y spread in bps — widening or narrowing vs yesterday? (higher spread = larger carry, EUR-supportive) - DE-JP 10Y spread in bps — widening or narrowing vs yesterday? - Carry Trade Assessment: the JPY is a premier funding currency for carry trades. When DE-JP yield spreads are wide, carry traders borrow cheap JPY and buy EUR-denominated assets — this is BULLISH EURJPY. When the BOJ hikes rates and JGB yields rise, this carry trade unwinds — BEARISH EURJPY. Assess current carry trade conditions: is the carry still attractive or is it unwinding? - Any notable JGB market moves (BOJ bond buying operations, yield cap breaches, JGB auction results) in the last 24h - Any BOJ bond purchase operations or policy signals affecting JGB yields in the last 24h - EUR/JPY Cross current level and today's directional move ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - JP 2Y JGB: [%] | DE 2Y Schatz: [%] | Spread (DE–JP): [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] - JP 10Y JGB: [%] | DE 10Y Bund: [%] | Spread (DE–JP): [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] - Carry Trade Status: [Attractive/Unwinding/Neutral] — brief reason - Notable JGB/BOJ Market Event: [describe or "None"] - Bond Market Bias: [EUR Favored / JPY Favored / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P2 - YIELD DIFFERENTIALS & CARRY TRADE (JGB vs BUND)] - JP 2Y JGB / DE 2Y Schatz: [% / %] | Spread (DE–JP): [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] - JP 10Y JGB / DE 10Y Bund: [% / %] | Spread (DE–JP): [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] - Carry Trade Status: [Attractive/Unwinding/Neutral] | Carry Logic: [one line reason] - Notable JGB/BOJ Event: [event or "None"] - Core Logic: [what the yield spread move + carry trade status implies for EURJPY today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - Eurozone: CPI (Headline + Core), PMI (Manufacturing + Services), GDP — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Japan — Priority Data Points (critical for BOJ policy decisions): * Japan National CPI (Headline + Core, ex-fresh food) — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous * Tokyo CPI (leading indicator for National CPI — released before National CPI) — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous * BOJ Tankan Survey (Business Conditions Index — Large Manufacturers + Large Non-Manufacturers) — latest reading and trend * Japan Wage Growth / Shunto Spring Wage Negotiations — latest result or status (CRITICAL: BOJ requires sustained wage growth before hiking; a strong Shunto outcome directly supports BOJ rate hikes and JPY strength) * Japan PMI (Manufacturing + Services) — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous * Japan GDP — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Citigroup Economic Surprise Index for EUR and JPY (exact values — positive = beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact Eurozone or Japan data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for EURJPY 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - EUR Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - JPY Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - Japan CPI (National): [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous or "Data Unavailable"] - Tokyo CPI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous or "Data Unavailable"] - BOJ Tankan (Large Mfg): [latest reading or "Data Unavailable"] - Shunto Wage Outcome: [result / status or "Data Unavailable"] | BOJ Hike Implication: [Supports/Neutral/Undermines] - Citi EUR Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Fundamental Upper Hand: [EUR / JPY / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC MOMENTUM (Eurozone vs Japan)] - EUR Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - JPY Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Japan CPI / Tokyo CPI: [figures or "Data Unavailable"] - BOJ Tankan / Shunto Wage: [readings or "Data Unavailable"] | BOJ Hike Implication: [Supports/Neutral/Undermines] - Citi EUR Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [which economy is outperforming and what it means for EURJPY, with emphasis on BOJ hike readiness] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest EURJPY and JPY/EUR positioning and sentiment data. Fetch and report: - CFTC COT report: NET Non-Commercial (speculative) JPY futures contracts — exact number, long or short. Is it at a historical extreme? JPY is historically one of the most heavily shorted currencies by speculative accounts. Use this data strictly for identifying the macro trend context and whether the smart money JPY short is gaining or fading. CRITICAL CHECK: if net JPY short positioning is at an extreme (e.g., historically large short position), flag the elevated risk of a JPY short-squeeze — a sudden BOJ hawkish surprise or a risk-off event can trigger a violent, rapid JPY rally (EURJPY selloff). Do NOT use COT for short-term intraday signals. - CFTC COT report: NET Non-Commercial (speculative) EUR futures contracts — exact number, long or short. Is it at a historical extreme? Use this for macro EUR trend context. - IG Client Sentiment: exact % of retail traders Long vs Short EURJPY right now. Weight this data higher for short-term and intraday contrarian setups — when retail is heavily skewed one way (e.g., 70%+ short), apply a contrarian lean for today's intraday bias. 📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — EURJPY (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for EURJPY. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant EURJPY options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - EURJPY NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - EURJPY Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish EURJPY — magnet above / Bearish EURJPY — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (EURJPY): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net JPY: [contracts + long/short] | Extreme Positioning: [Yes/No] | Short-Squeeze Risk: [High/Medium/Low] - CFTC Net EUR: [contracts + long/short] | Macro Trend Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral macro positioning] - IG Retail EURJPY: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On / Risk-Off] | EURJPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Contrarian] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT & POSITIONING] - CFTC Net JPY: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Short-Squeeze Risk: [High/Medium/Low] - CFTC Net EUR: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Macro Context: [trend direction] - IG Retail Sentiment EURJPY: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off] | EURJPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish] - Core Logic: [contrarian intraday signal from IG + macro trend from COT + JPY short-squeeze risk — which edge applies today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Contrarian] ```
phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest macro risks, global risk sentiment, and Japanese FX intervention signals affecting EURJPY. Fetch and report: - GLOBAL RISK SENTIMENT (CRITICAL for JPY as a safe-haven): JPY is a premier safe-haven currency. Assess the current global risk environment: * Risk-On (equity markets rising, VIX falling, credit spreads tightening) = BAD for JPY = BULLISH EURJPY * Risk-Off (equity markets falling, VIX spiking, credit spreads widening, geopolitical stress) = GOOD for JPY = BEARISH EURJPY State clearly: Risk-On or Risk-Off, and the EURJPY directional implication. - OIL/ENERGY PRICES — Japan is a massive energy importer with virtually no domestic oil production. High oil prices increase Japan's import bill, widen the trade deficit, and WEAKEN JPY (BULLISH EURJPY). Falling oil prices reduce import costs and SUPPORT JPY (BEARISH EURJPY). Report: * Brent Crude price (exact level) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%) * WTI Crude price (exact level) * Natural Gas price (exact level) * EURJPY implication of current oil move: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral EURJPY] - MoF / BOJ FX INTERVENTION RISK (CRITICAL — Japan-specific): * Check current USD/JPY and EUR/JPY levels. The Japanese Ministry of Finance (MoF) has historically intervened in FX markets to defend the JPY when USD/JPY reaches extreme levels (historically above 145–155 USD/JPY). Search for any verbal warnings ("verbal intervention") from Japanese officials (MoF, BOJ, Finance Minister, Vice Finance Minister for International Affairs). * Has the MoF or BOJ recently conducted actual FX intervention (buying JPY / selling USD or EUR)? If so, when and at what level? * Is the current USD/JPY or EUR/JPY level in the "danger zone" where intervention becomes a credible risk? * Intervention Risk Rating: [Low / Moderate / High / Imminent] * State clearly: does intervention risk cap the upside for EURJPY right now? - Gold (XAUUSD) current price, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and safe-haven context — is Gold rising (risk-off, which also supports JPY) or falling (risk-on, which also weakens JPY)? - Any EU or Japan political / fiscal headlines in the last 24 hours - Any BOJ, ECB, or MoF speeches or major scheduled events TODAY that could spike EURJPY volatility - Rate the overall risk environment: Low / Medium / High / Extreme ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Global Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off] | EURJPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Brent Crude: [price] | WTI: [price] | Natural Gas: [price] | Oil→JPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral EURJPY] - Gold (XAUUSD): [price] | Move: [Up/Down X%] | Safe-Haven Signal: [Active Risk-Off / Risk-On / Neutral] | EURJPY Correlation: [Supports/Contradicts/Neutral] - USD/JPY Level: [level] | EUR/JPY Level: [level] - MoF/BOJ Intervention Risk: [Low/Moderate/High/Imminent] | Verbal Warning Detected: [Yes/No — quote if Yes] | Actual Intervention: [Yes/No — date if Yes] - Intervention Upside Cap on EURJPY: [Yes/No — brief reason] - Top Risk Event Today: [name + estimated EURJPY impact] - Risk Environment: [Low/Medium/High/Extreme] - Bias Override Risk: [Yes/No] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P5 - MACRO, RISK SENTIMENT & INTERVENTION RISK] - Global Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off] | EURJPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Brent Crude: [price] | WTI: [price] | Natural Gas: [price] | Oil→JPY Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Gold (XAUUSD): [price] | Move: [direction %] | Safe-Haven Flow: [Active/Neutral/Absent] | Bias Correlation: [Supports/Contradicts/Neutral] - USD/JPY: [level] | EUR/JPY: [level] - MoF/BOJ Intervention Risk: [Low/Moderate/High/Imminent] | Verbal Warning: [Yes/No] | Actual Intervention: [Yes/No] - Intervention Upside Cap: [Yes/No — brief reason] - Top Risk Event Today: [name + direction of impact on EURJPY] - Risk Environment Rating: [Low / Medium / High / Extreme] - Core Logic: [does risk sentiment + oil + intervention risk support, dampen, or override the fundamental bias?] - Bias Override: [Yes / No] ```
phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear EURJPY institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Policy Divergence (BOJ vs ECB): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Yield Differentials & Carry Trade (JGB vs Bund): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Momentum (Eurozone vs Japan): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & JPY Positioning (COT + IG): [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro, Risk Sentiment & MoF/BOJ Intervention Risk: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Pay special attention to Pillar 5 (Intervention Risk): if MoF/BOJ intervention risk is rated High or Imminent, this must be noted as a hard cap on EURJPY upside and should lower confidence for any Bullish bias.
- Pay special attention to Pillar 4 (JPY Short-Squeeze Risk): if CFTC net JPY short is at an extreme, flag that a short-squeeze is a tail risk that could violently reverse any Bullish EURJPY bias.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity. Note that Tokyo session is key for JPY pairs.
